---
title: "NMI Paper Figures — Evidence-Based Context Engineering"
author: "Bianchi & Schokker"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    theme: flatly
params:
  output_dir: "figures_nmi"
  format: "tiff"
  dpi: 300
  width_mm: 180
  height_mm: 120
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE,
  fig.retina = 2,
  dev = if (requireNamespace("ragg", quietly = TRUE)) "ragg_png" else "png"
)

library(tidyverse)
library(jsonlite)
library(scales)
library(patchwork)
library(ggrepel)
library(here)

# Output directory for TIFF/PNG figures
fig_dir <- here::here("publication", "figures")
dir.create(fig_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# Helper to save figures as both TIFF and PDF.
#
# NMI submission format rules (verified 2026-04-09 against
# nature.com/natmachintell/submission-guidelines/aip-and-formatting and
# research-figure-guide.nature.com/figures/extended-data-formatting-guidelines/):
#
#   - Main figures (Fig 1-6):
#       Width   <= 180 mm  (single col 88 mm or double col 180 mm)
#       Height  <= 247 mm  (depending on caption length)
#       Format  -> PDF / EPS / AI (vector, PREFERRED for ggplot output)
#                  TIFF also accepted but rasterised
#       Size    <= 50 MB
#
#   - Extended Data figures (ED Fig 1-6):
#       Width   <= 180 mm
#       Height  <= 170 mm  (figure + legend must fit a single PDF page)
#       Format  -> JPEG / TIFF / EPS ONLY  (PDF is NOT accepted by the
#                  ED submission pipeline; the .pdf files emitted here are
#                  for in-repo review/preview only and must NOT be uploaded
#                  to the NMI portal as ED submissions)
#       Size    <= 10 MB per item
#
# Practical upload guidance:
#   - For Fig 1-6 in the NMI portal, upload the .pdf files written here.
#   - For ED Fig 1-6 in the NMI portal, upload the .tiff files written here.
#
# TIFFs use LZW compression so chart-style content (mostly background +
# line art) compresses 95-99% — every ED .tiff stays well under 1 MB.
save_figure <- function(plot, filename, w = params$width_mm, h = params$height_mm) {
  # TIFF — required submission format for Extended Data, also accepted
  # (but not preferred) for main figures.
  tiff_path <- file.path(fig_dir, paste0(filename, ".tiff"))
  ggsave(tiff_path, plot = plot, width = w, height = h, units = "mm",
         dpi = params$dpi, bg = "white", compression = "lzw")
  # PDF — preferred submission format for main figures (vector). NOT
  # accepted for ED submissions: keep this output for in-repo preview
  # via paper_figures.html and as the source for the figures embedded
  # in the manuscript draft.
  pdf_path <- file.path(fig_dir, paste0(filename, ".pdf"))
  ggsave(pdf_path, plot = plot, width = w, height = h, units = "mm",
         bg = "white")
  cat(sprintf("Saved: %s + .pdf (%d x %d mm, %d dpi)\n", tiff_path, w, h, params$dpi))
  invisible(tiff_path)
}
```

```{r palette-and-theme}
# --- Colorblind-safe palette (Wong 2011, Nat. Methods 8, 441) ---
# Diverging triple: vermillion / yellow / bluish-green
col_bad    <- "#D55E00"  # Wong vermillion
col_mid    <- "#F0E442"  # Wong yellow
col_good   <- "#009E73"  # Wong bluish green
col_info   <- "#0072B2"  # Wong blue
col_muted  <- "#666666"  # neutral grey

# Categorical: Wong 2011 base 8 + Tol "muted" extension for >8 categories
# (all colorblind-friendly, contrast-tested)
model_palette <- c(
  "#E69F00", "#56B4E9", "#009E73", "#F0E442",
  "#0072B2", "#D55E00", "#CC79A7", "#000000",
  "#332288", "#117733", "#88CCEE", "#DDCC77",
  "#CC6677", "#AA4499", "#882255", "#44AA99",
  "#999933", "#661100", "#6699CC", "#888888"
)

# Tick label formatter: turn 0-1 fraction into 0-100 integer.
# Use this on axes whose title already contains "(%)" so the tick
# labels do not redundantly print the "%" symbol.
pct100 <- function(x) round(x * 100)

config_palette <- c(
  "single_pass"   = "#56B4E9",
  "multi_hop"     = "#E69F00",
  "rlm_5"         = "#009E73",
  "rlm_10"        = "#0072B2",
  "rlm_20"        = "#CC79A7",
  "verified_pass" = "#D55E00"
)

config_labels <- c(
  "single_pass"   = "Single Pass",
  "multi_hop"     = "Multi-Hop",
  "rlm_5"         = "RLM-5",
  "rlm_10"        = "RLM-10",
  "rlm_20"        = "RLM-20",
  "verified_pass" = "Verified Pass"
)

config_order <- c("single_pass", "multi_hop", "rlm_5", "rlm_10", "rlm_20", "verified_pass")

# NMI-compliant theme (Nature Machine Intelligence submission guidelines):
#   - Font: Arial/Helvetica (sans-serif)
#   - Body text 5-7 pt; panel labels 8 pt bold lowercase
#   - No background gridlines; no 3D/patterns/drop shadows
#   - Colorblind-accessible palette (Wong 2011)
theme_nmi <- function(base_size = 7) {
  font_family <- if ("Helvetica" %in% names(grDevices::postscriptFonts())) "Helvetica" else "sans"
  theme_minimal(base_size = base_size, base_family = font_family) +
    theme(
      # Panel labels (a, b, c) at 8 pt bold lowercase, left-aligned to plot
      plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 8, hjust = 0,
                                       margin = margin(b = 3)),
      plot.subtitle    = element_text(color = col_muted, size = 6,
                                       margin = margin(b = 3)),
      plot.title.position = "plot",
      # Axes: 7 pt titles, 6 pt tick labels
      axis.title       = element_text(size = 7),
      axis.text        = element_text(size = 6, color = "grey20"),
      axis.line        = element_line(linewidth = 0.3, color = "black"),
      axis.ticks       = element_line(linewidth = 0.25, color = "grey40"),
      # Legend
      legend.position  = "bottom",
      legend.title     = element_text(size = 7, face = "bold"),
      legend.text      = element_text(size = 6),
      legend.key.size  = unit(3, "mm"),
      # Facet strips: 7 pt bold (NMI disallows decorative strips)
      strip.text       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 7),
      strip.background = element_blank(),
      # No gridlines (NMI: "Avoid background gridlines")
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      # Margins
      plot.margin      = margin(4, 4, 4, 4)
    )
}
```

```{r load-data}
# --- JSON utilities ---
fix_json <- function(path) {
  text <- readLines(path, warn = FALSE) |> paste(collapse = "\n")
  gsub(": NaN", ": null", text) |> fromJSON(flatten = TRUE)
}

safe_load <- function(path) {
  if (file.exists(path)) fix_json(path) else NULL
}

short_name <- function(x) {
  x |>
    str_remove("^ollama::") |>
    str_remove("^gemini::") |>
    str_remove(":latest$") |>
    str_replace("gemini-3-flash-preview", "gemini-3-flash")
}

# Canonical model naming: enforces consistent `family:size[b]` format across all figures.
# Handles hyphenated, colon, and [think:X] suffixed inputs from all data sources.
canonical_model_name <- function(x) {
  # Step 1: strip backend prefixes
  x <- x |>
    str_remove("^ollama::") |>
    str_remove("^gemini::")

  # Step 2: separate the think-mode suffix (if any) from the model base
  think_suffix <- str_extract(x, "[_\\s]?\\[?think[:=-]\\w+\\]?$|[_\\s]\\+think$")
  base <- if (!is.na(think_suffix)) str_remove(x, stringr::fixed(think_suffix)) else x

  # Normalise think suffix to " [think:X]"
  think_norm <- ""
  if (!is.na(think_suffix)) {
    m <- str_match(think_suffix, "think[:=-]?(\\w+)")
    mode <- if (!is.na(m[1,2])) m[1,2] else "on"
    if (mode %in% c("True", "true", "TRUE", "on", "On")) mode <- "on"
    if (mode %in% c("False", "false", "FALSE")) mode <- "off"
    if (grepl("\\+think", think_suffix)) mode <- "on"
    if (mode != "off") think_norm <- paste0(" [think:", mode, "]")
  }

  # Step 3: canonicalise the base model name
  base <- base |>
    # Canonicalise Gemini preview → gemini-3-flash
    str_replace("gemini-3-flash-preview", "gemini-3-flash") |>
    str_replace("^Gemini$", "gemini-3-flash") |>
    # Resolve models with ambiguous tags to explicit sizes
    str_replace("qwen3-coder-next[:-]q4[_ ]K[_ ]M", "qwen3-coder-next:80b") |>
    str_replace("qwen3-coder-next-q4_K_M", "qwen3-coder-next:80b") |>
    str_replace("qwen3-coder:latest", "qwen3-coder:30b") |>
    str_replace("qwen3-coder-latest", "qwen3-coder:30b") |>
    str_replace("^qwen3-coder$", "qwen3-coder:30b") |>
    str_replace("glm-4\\.7-flash:latest", "glm-4.7-flash:30b") |>
    str_replace("glm-4\\.7-flash-bf16", "glm-4.7-flash:30b") |>
    str_replace("glm-4\\.7-flash:bf16", "glm-4.7-flash:30b") |>
    # Normalise hyphen-before-size → colon-before-size
    # Apply to well-known model families
    str_replace("^(nemotron-3-nano)-(\\d+b)$", "\\1:\\2") |>
    str_replace("^(devstral-small-2)-(\\d+b)$", "\\1:\\2") |>
    str_replace("^(gemma3)-(\\d+b)$", "\\1:\\2") |>
    str_replace("^(llama3)-(\\d+b)$", "\\1:\\2") |>
    str_replace("^(gpt-oss)-(\\d+b)$", "\\1:\\2") |>
    str_replace("^(deepseek-r1)-(\\d+b)$", "\\1:\\2") |>
    str_replace("^(olmo-3)-(\\d+b)$", "\\1:\\2") |>
    str_replace("^(qwen3\\.5)-(\\d+b)$", "\\1:\\2")

  paste0(base, think_norm)
}
canonical_model_name <- Vectorize(canonical_model_name, USE.NAMES = FALSE)

results_dir <- here::here("publication", "results")

# --- V4-4 Generation Strategy ---
data <- fix_json(file.path(results_dir, "exp04_generation_latest.json"))
pqr_tbl <- data$per_question_results |> as_tibble()
# Ensure optional columns exist (OOD fields may be missing in answerable-only rows)
for (col in c("judge_scores.refusal_accuracy", "citation_metrics.total_citations",
              "citation_metrics.source_coverage")) {
  if (!col %in% names(pqr_tbl)) pqr_tbl[[col]] <- NA_real_
}
pqr <- pqr_tbl |>
  mutate(
    config       = factor(config, levels = config_order),
    config_label = factor(config_labels[as.character(config)], levels = config_labels),
    is_rlm       = grepl("^rlm_", config),
    category     = factor(category),
    correctness  = judge_scores.correctness,
    completeness = judge_scores.completeness,
    faithfulness = judge_scores.faithfulness,
    citation_quality = judge_scores.citation_quality,
    refusal_accuracy = judge_scores.refusal_accuracy,
    passed       = ifelse(answerable, !is.na(correctness) & correctness >= 3, NA),
    latency_s    = generation.latency_s,
    llm_calls    = generation.llm_calls,
    total_citations = citation_metrics.total_citations,
    source_coverage = citation_metrics.source_coverage
  )

ans   <- pqr |> filter(answerable == TRUE)
unans <- pqr |> filter(answerable == FALSE)

config_summary <- ans |>
  group_by(config, config_label) |>
  summarise(
    n = n(), pass_rate = mean(passed, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_corr = mean(correctness, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_comp = mean(completeness, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_faith = mean(faithfulness, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_cite = mean(citation_quality, na.rm = TRUE),
    med_latency = median(latency_s, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_citations = mean(total_citations, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_coverage = mean(source_coverage, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# --- Deterministic vs Judge ---
det_files <- list.files(results_dir, pattern = "analysis_deterministic_.*\\.json$", full.names = TRUE)
det_file  <- sort(det_files, decreasing = TRUE)[1]
det_data  <- fix_json(det_file)
det <- det_data$per_result |>
  as_tibble() |>
  mutate(
    config = factor(config, levels = config_order),
    config_label = factor(config_labels[as.character(config)], levels = config_labels),
    det_correctness      = deterministic_scores.correctness,
    det_completeness     = deterministic_scores.completeness,
    det_faithfulness     = deterministic_scores.faithfulness,
    det_citation_quality = deterministic_scores.citation_quality,
    judge_correctness    = judge_scores.correctness,
    judge_completeness   = judge_scores.completeness,
    judge_faithfulness   = judge_scores.faithfulness,
    judge_citation_quality = judge_scores.citation_quality
  )
det_ans <- det |> filter(answerable == TRUE)

# Extract objective metrics (concept recall, semantic similarity) for Fig. 2
# fromJSON(flatten=TRUE) creates dotted column names
det_ans <- det_ans |>
  mutate(
    concept_recall       = objective_metrics.concept_recall,
    semantic_similarity  = objective_metrics.semantic_similarity,
    citation_density     = objective_metrics.citation_density
  )

det_summary <- det_ans |>
  group_by(config, config_label) |>
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    det_corr = mean(det_correctness, na.rm = TRUE),
    det_faith = mean(det_faithfulness, na.rm = TRUE),
    judge_corr = mean(judge_correctness, na.rm = TRUE),
    judge_faith = mean(judge_faithfulness, na.rm = TRUE),
    det_mean = mean((det_correctness + det_completeness + det_faithfulness + det_citation_quality) / 4, na.rm = TRUE),
    judge_mean = mean((judge_correctness + judge_completeness + judge_faithfulness + judge_citation_quality) / 4, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# --- MiniCheck ---
mc_files <- list.files(results_dir, pattern = "exp04_minicheck_.*\\.json$", full.names = TRUE)
if (length(mc_files) == 0)
  mc_files <- list.files(results_dir, pattern = "analysis_minicheck_.*\\.json$", full.names = TRUE)
mc_file <- sort(mc_files, decreasing = TRUE)[1]
has_minicheck <- !is.na(mc_file) && file.exists(mc_file)
if (has_minicheck) {
  mc_data <- fix_json(mc_file)
  mc <- mc_data$per_result |>
    as_tibble() |>
    mutate(
      config = factor(config, levels = config_order),
      config_label = factor(config_labels[as.character(config)], levels = config_labels),
      mc_precision = minicheck.precision,
      mc_recall = minicheck.recall,
      mc_f1 = minicheck.f1,
      judge_faith = judge_scores.faithfulness
    )
  mc_summary <- mc |>
    group_by(config, config_label) |>
    summarise(
      n = n(), Precision = mean(mc_precision, na.rm = TRUE),
      Recall = mean(mc_recall, na.rm = TRUE), F1 = mean(mc_f1, na.rm = TRUE),
      `Judge Faith.` = mean(judge_faith, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop"
    )
}

# --- Paper Validation (TPR/TNR, Kendall's W, ECE) ---
val_data <- safe_load(file.path(results_dir, "analysis_paper_validation.json"))
has_validation <- !is.null(val_data)

# --- Saturation / Per-turn ---
pt_raw <- safe_load(file.path(results_dir, "analysis_perturn_analysis_latest.json"))
has_perturn <- !is.null(pt_raw)

# --- Flip Rate ---
fr_raw <- safe_load(file.path(results_dir, "analysis_fliprate_analysis.json"))
has_fliprate <- !is.null(fr_raw)

# --- V4-1/2/3 Retrieval ---
v41 <- safe_load(file.path(results_dir, "exp01_embedding_latest.json"))
v42 <- safe_load(file.path(results_dir, "exp02_search_latest.json"))
v43a <- safe_load(file.path(results_dir, "exp03a_chunking_latest.json"))
v43b <- safe_load(file.path(results_dir, "exp03b_scalability_latest.json"))

# --- V4-0 Model Selection ---
triage_data <- safe_load(file.path(results_dir, "exp00_triage_latest.json"))
obj_data    <- safe_load(file.path(results_dir, "exp00_objective_latest.json"))
cal_data    <- safe_load(file.path(results_dir, "exp00_calibration_latest.json"))

# --- V4-5 Scaling ---
v45_raw <- safe_load(file.path(results_dir, "exp05_scaling_latest.json"))
has_v45 <- !is.null(v45_raw)

if (has_v45) {
  v45_tbl <- v45_raw$per_question_results |> as_tibble()
  # Add missing columns with NA if they don't exist (OOD vs answerable have different judge fields)
  if (!"judge_scores.refusal_accuracy" %in% names(v45_tbl))
    v45_tbl$judge_scores.refusal_accuracy <- NA_real_
  if (!"generation.error" %in% names(v45_tbl))
    v45_tbl$generation.error <- NA_character_
  if (!"judge_scores.correctness" %in% names(v45_tbl))
    v45_tbl$judge_scores.correctness <- NA_real_
  if (!"generation.latency_s" %in% names(v45_tbl))
    v45_tbl$generation.latency_s <- NA_real_

  v45 <- v45_tbl |>
    mutate(
      index_id    = index_name,
      core_size   = as.integer(str_extract(index_name, "(?<=_s)\\d+")),
      noise_ratio = as.integer(str_extract(index_name, "(?<=_n)\\d+")),
      config      = factor(config, levels = config_order),
      config_label = factor(config_labels[as.character(config)], levels = config_labels),
      correctness = judge_scores.correctness,
      pass        = !is.na(correctness) & correctness >= 3,
      latency_s   = generation.latency_s,
      refusal_accuracy = judge_scores.refusal_accuracy,
      gen_error   = generation.error
    )

  # Flag broken results: 429 in answer or explicit error
  v45 <- v45 |>
    mutate(
      answer_text = as.character(generation.answer),
      is_quota_error = str_detect(answer_text, fixed("429")) |
                       str_detect(answer_text, fixed("RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED")),
      is_broken = is_quota_error | (!is.na(gen_error) & gen_error != ""),
      is_broken = replace_na(is_broken, FALSE)
    )

  n_broken <- sum(v45$is_broken)
  n_clean  <- sum(!v45$is_broken)
  cat(sprintf("V4-5 data: %d results (%d clean, %d broken/error)\n",
    nrow(v45), n_clean, n_broken))

  # Clean subset: exclude broken results
  v45_clean <- v45 |> filter(!is_broken)
} else {
  cat("V4-5 scaling results not yet available.\n")
}

# --- V4-6 Pipeline Ablation ---
v46_raw <- safe_load(file.path(results_dir, "exp06_orchestration_ablation_latest.json"))
has_v46 <- !is.null(v46_raw)

if (has_v46) {
  v46_pqr <- v46_raw$per_question_results |> as_tibble()
  v46_answerable <- v46_pqr |> filter(answerable == TRUE)
  v46_unanswerable <- v46_pqr |> filter(answerable == FALSE)

  v46_pass <- v46_answerable |>
    group_by(config) |>
    summarise(
      pass_rate = mean(judge_scores.correctness >= 3, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
      mean_correctness = mean(judge_scores.correctness, na.rm = TRUE),
      n_answerable = n(), .groups = "drop"
    )

  v46_refusal <- v46_unanswerable |>
    group_by(config) |>
    summarise(
      refusal_rate = mean(judge_scores.refusal_accuracy >= 3, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
      n_unanswerable = n(), .groups = "drop"
    )

  v46_latency <- v46_pqr |>
    group_by(config) |>
    summarise(
      median_latency = median(generation.latency_s, na.rm = TRUE),
      n_total = n(), .groups = "drop"
    )

  v46_summary <- v46_pass |>
    left_join(v46_refusal, by = "config") |>
    left_join(v46_latency, by = "config")

  cat(sprintf("V4-6 data: %d results across %d configs\n",
    nrow(v46_pqr), n_distinct(v46_pqr$config)))
} else {
  cat("V4-6 ablation results not yet available.\n")
}

# --- V4-8 Code Generation ---
v48_comp   <- safe_load(file.path(results_dir, "exp08_comparison_v2_vs_v4.json"))
has_v48    <- !is.null(v48_comp)
v48_files  <- list.files(results_dir, pattern = "exp08_coder_benchmark_.*_latest\\.json", full.names = TRUE)
v48_models <- list()
for (f in v48_files) {
  d <- safe_load(f)
  if (!is.null(d) && !is.null(d$summary)) {
    think_tag <- d$think %||% "off"
    key <- paste0(d$gen_model %||% basename(f),
                  if (think_tag != "off") paste0("[think:", think_tag, "]") else "")
    v48_models[[key]] <- d
  }
}
has_v48_models <- length(v48_models) > 0

cat("All data loaded.\n")
```

---

# Figure 2 — Generation Strategy Comparison {.tabset}

```{r fig2-build, fig.width=10, fig.height=10}
# Panel a: Pass rate
p2a <- config_summary |>
  ggplot(aes(x = config_label, y = pass_rate, fill = config)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.8, linetype = "dashed", color = col_muted, linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = percent(pass_rate, accuracy = 0.1)),
            vjust = -0.5, size = 2.8, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = config_palette, guide = "none") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = pct100, limits = c(0, 1.05), expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))) +
  labs(title = "a",
       x = NULL, y = "Pass rate (%)") +
  theme_nmi() + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Panel b: Concept recall (deterministic ground truth)
cr_summary <- det_ans |>
  group_by(config, config_label) |>
  summarise(mean_cr = mean(concept_recall, na.rm = TRUE),
            se_cr = sd(concept_recall, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
            .groups = "drop")

p2b <- cr_summary |>
  ggplot(aes(x = config_label, y = mean_cr, fill = config)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_cr - se_cr, ymax = mean_cr + se_cr), width = 0.25, linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", mean_cr * 100), y = mean_cr + se_cr),
            vjust = -0.5, size = 2.8, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = config_palette, guide = "none") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = pct100, limits = c(0, 1.0), expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.08))) +
  labs(title = "b",
       x = NULL, y = "Concept recall (%)") +
  theme_nmi() + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Panel c: Latency vs quality bubble plot
p2c <- config_summary |>
  ggplot(aes(x = med_latency, y = mean_corr, color = config, size = pass_rate)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = config_label), size = 2.5, show.legend = FALSE,
                  max.overlaps = 20, min.segment.length = 0,
                  box.padding = 1.0, point.padding = 0.8, force = 6,
                  force_pull = 0.2, seed = 42) +
  scale_color_manual(values = config_palette, guide = "none") +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(3, 10), labels = pct100, name = "Pass rate (%)") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 115), breaks = seq(0, 100, 25)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(2.5, 4.5), breaks = seq(2.5, 4.5, 0.5)) +
  labs(title = "c",
       x = "Median latency (s)", y = "Score") +
  theme_nmi() + theme(legend.position = "right",
                       legend.key.size = unit(0.35, "cm"),
                       legend.text = element_text(size = 7),
                       axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 2, b = 0)),
                       plot.margin = margin(4, 4, 0, 4))

# Panel d: OOD refusal rate
refusal_summary <- unans |>
  group_by(config, config_label) |>
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    refusal_rate = mean(!is.na(refusal_accuracy) & refusal_accuracy >= 3, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

p2d <- refusal_summary |>
  ggplot(aes(x = config_label, y = refusal_rate, fill = config)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = percent(refusal_rate, accuracy = 0.1)),
            vjust = -0.5, size = 2.8, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = config_palette, guide = "none") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = pct100, limits = c(0, 1.05), expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))) +
  labs(title = "d",
       x = NULL, y = "Refusal rate (%)") +
  theme_nmi() + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Panel e: Outcome flips (answerable questions only, threshold >= 3)
# Compute directly from per-question results to ensure answerable-only denominator
sp_ans <- ans |> filter(config == "single_pass") |>
  select(question_id, sp_passed = passed)

flip_df_2e <- ans |>
  filter(config != "single_pass") |>
  inner_join(sp_ans, by = "question_id") |>
  group_by(config, config_label) |>
  summarise(
    harmful = sum(sp_passed & !passed, na.rm = TRUE),   # correct -> incorrect
    helpful = sum(!sp_passed & passed, na.rm = TRUE),   # incorrect -> correct
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    total_flips = harmful + helpful,
    harmful_rate = harmful / pmax(total_flips, 1),
    strategy = config_label |>
      str_replace("Single Pass", "Single pass") |>
      str_replace("Verified Pass", "Verified pass"),
    strategy = factor(strategy, levels = rev(unique(strategy)))
  )

flip_long_2e <- flip_df_2e |>
  pivot_longer(c(harmful, helpful), names_to = "type", values_to = "count") |>
  mutate(count_plot = ifelse(type == "harmful", -count, count))

p2e <- ggplot(flip_long_2e, aes(x = count_plot, y = strategy, fill = type)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = abs(count)),
            hjust = ifelse(flip_long_2e$type == "harmful", 1.3, -0.3),
            size = 2.8, fontface = "bold") +
  geom_text(data = flip_df_2e,
            aes(x = max(flip_df_2e$helpful) + 4, y = strategy,
                label = sprintf("%.0f%%", harmful_rate * 100)),
            inherit.aes = FALSE, size = 2.5, hjust = 0, color = col_muted) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "grey30", linewidth = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("harmful" = col_bad, "helpful" = col_good),
                    labels = c("Harmful (correct\u2192incorrect)", "Helpful (incorrect\u2192correct)")) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = function(x) abs(x)) +
  labs(title = "e",
       x = "Flipped questions", y = NULL, fill = NULL) +
  theme_nmi() + theme(panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
                       legend.position = "bottom",
                       legend.key.size = unit(0.3, "cm"))

# Assemble: 3 rows — (a, b), (c, d), (e)
top_row <- (p2a | p2b) + plot_layout(axes = "collect_x")
mid_row <- (free(p2c, type = "label", side = "b") | p2d)

fig2 <- (top_row / mid_row / free(p2e)) +
  plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1, 0.8)) +
  plot_annotation(
    theme = theme_nmi() + theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold", margin = margin(b = 4)))
  )

fig2
save_figure(fig2, "fig2_generation_strategy", w = 180, h = 240)
```

---

# Figure 3 — Scaling Behavior: Depth and Corpus Size {.tabset}

Merged figure: saturation (V4-4/V4-9) + corpus scaling (V4-5).
Source coverage (old Fig 3b) moved to Extended Data.

```{r fig3-build, fig.width=12, fig.height=12, eval=has_perturn}
# ── Shared: load V4-9 data and fit logistic models ──
library(minpack.lm)

ylim_sat <- c(0, 0.70)  # shared y-axis for panels a and b

# V4-9: load ALL models (Gemini + local) from merged files
v49_files <- list.files(results_dir,
                        pattern = "exp09_depth_sweep_.*_merged\\.json$", full.names = TRUE)
v49_all <- map_dfr(v49_files, function(f) {
  d <- fromJSON(f, flatten = TRUE)
  model_name <- gsub("ollama::", "", d$model %||% gsub(".*sweep_(.*)_merged\\.json", "\\1", basename(f)))
  model_name <- gsub("_", " ", model_name)
  d$results |> as_tibble() |> mutate(model = model_name)
}) |>
  filter(!is.na(concept_recall))

# Separate Gemini and local models
v49_gemini <- v49_all |>
  filter(grepl("gemini", model, ignore.case = TRUE)) |>
  group_by(model, depth) |>
  summarise(mean_cr = mean(concept_recall), se = sd(concept_recall)/sqrt(n()),
            n = n(), .groups = "drop") |>
  mutate(ci_lower = mean_cr - 1.96*se, ci_upper = mean_cr + 1.96*se,
         model = "gemini-3-flash")

v49_data <- v49_all |>
  filter(!grepl("gemini", model, ignore.case = TRUE)) |>
  group_by(model, depth) |>
  summarise(mean_cr = mean(concept_recall), se = sd(concept_recall)/sqrt(n()),
            n = n(), .groups = "drop") |>
  mutate(ci_lower = mean_cr - 1.96*se, ci_upper = mean_cr + 1.96*se)

# Model spec for local models (panel b) — all 8 local models from V4-9
local_spec <- tribble(
  ~model_key,                  ~display,                   ~params_B, ~color,
  "qwen3-coder-next:q4 K M",  "qwen3-coder-next:80b",     80,        "#009E73",
  "qwen3-coder:latest",       "qwen3-coder:30b",          30,        "#0072B2",
  "nemotron-3-nano:30b",      "nemotron-3-nano:30b",      30,        "#E69F00",
  "gemma3:27b",               "gemma3:27b",               27,        "#CC79A7",
  "devstral-small-2:24b",     "devstral-small-2:24b",     24,        "#56B4E9",
  "gpt-oss:20b",              "gpt-oss:20b",              20,        "#D55E00",
  "glm-4.7-flash:latest",     "glm-4.7-flash:30b",       30,        "#F0E442",
  "llama3:8b",                "llama3:8b",                 8,        "#999999",
)

# Fit logistic to all models (Gemini + 8 local)
all_models_spec <- bind_rows(
  tibble(model_key = "gemini-3-flash", display = "gemini-3-flash", params_B = NA_real_, color = "grey40"),
  local_spec
)

fit_types <- c("Logistic", "Sat. Exp.", "Logarithmic", "Linear")
r2_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = nrow(all_models_spec), ncol = length(fit_types),
                    dimnames = list(all_models_spec$display, fit_types))
logistic_params <- list()
d_seq <- seq(0.8, 55, by = 0.2)

for (i in seq_len(nrow(all_models_spec))) {
  mk <- all_models_spec$model_key[i]
  dn <- all_models_spec$display[i]
  mdata <- if (mk == "gemini-3-flash") v49_gemini else v49_data |> filter(model == mk)
  if (nrow(mdata) < 3) next
  d <- mdata$depth; cr <- mdata$mean_cr

  # Linear
  tryCatch({ fit <- lm(cr ~ d); r2_matrix[dn, "Linear"] <- 1 - sum(residuals(fit)^2)/sum((cr-mean(cr))^2) }, error=function(e){})
  # Logarithmic
  tryCatch({ fit <- lm(cr ~ log(d)); r2_matrix[dn, "Logarithmic"] <- 1 - sum(residuals(fit)^2)/sum((cr-mean(cr))^2) }, error=function(e){})
  # Saturating exponential
  tryCatch({ fit <- nlsLM(cr ~ L*(1-exp(-k*d)), start=list(L=max(cr)*1.1, k=0.3), lower=c(0.01,0.001), upper=c(1,10), control=nls.lm.control(maxiter=200)); r2_matrix[dn, "Sat. Exp."] <- 1-sum(residuals(fit)^2)/sum((cr-mean(cr))^2) }, error=function(e){})
  # Logistic
  tryCatch({
    fit <- nlsLM(cr ~ L/(1+exp(-k*(d-d0))), data=mdata, start=list(L=max(cr)*1.1, k=0.5, d0=median(d)), lower=c(0.01,0.01,-10), upper=c(1.5,5,50), control=nls.lm.control(maxiter=500))
    ss_res <- sum(residuals(fit)^2); ss_tot <- sum((cr-mean(cr))^2)
    r2_matrix[dn, "Logistic"] <- 1 - ss_res/ss_tot
    L_val <- coef(fit)["L"]; k_val <- coef(fit)["k"]; d0_val <- coef(fit)["d0"]
    logistic_params[[dn]] <- list(L=L_val, k=k_val, d0=d0_val, r2=1-ss_res/ss_tot,
                                   curve=tibble(depth=d_seq, cr=L_val/(1+exp(-k_val*(d_seq-d0_val)))))
  }, error=function(e){})
}

# ── Panel a: Gemini (10 depths from V4-9) ──
gemini_curve <- logistic_params[["gemini-3-flash"]]$curve
gemini_p <- logistic_params[["gemini-3-flash"]]
gemini_label_1 <- sprintf("italic(L)==%.2f*','~~italic(k)==%.2f*','~~italic(d)[0]==%.1f", gemini_p$L, gemini_p$k, gemini_p$d0)
gemini_label_2 <- sprintf("italic(R)^2==%.3f", gemini_p$r2)

# Compute 80%-of-gain depth for subtitle
gemini_80pct_depth <- v49_gemini |>
  mutate(gain_frac = (mean_cr - min(mean_cr)) / (max(mean_cr) - min(mean_cr))) |>
  filter(gain_frac >= 0.8) |> slice_min(depth) |> pull(depth)

p3a <- ggplot() +
  annotate("rect", xmin=0.5, xmax=gemini_80pct_depth, ymin=-Inf, ymax=Inf, fill=col_good, alpha=0.15) +
  annotate("text", x=gemini_80pct_depth/1.5, y=0.67, label="80% of gain", size=2, color="#009E73", fontface="italic") +
  geom_line(data=gemini_curve, aes(x=depth, y=cr), color="grey40", linewidth=1) +
  geom_errorbar(data=v49_gemini, aes(x=depth, ymin=ci_lower, ymax=ci_upper), width=0.5, color="grey40", alpha=0.5) +
  geom_point(data=v49_gemini, aes(x=depth, y=mean_cr), size=3, shape=21, fill="white", color="grey40", stroke=1.2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept=max(v49_gemini$mean_cr), linetype="dotted", color=col_muted) +
  annotate("text", x=1.5, y=0.10, label=gemini_label_1, parse=TRUE, size=2.5, color="grey40", hjust=0) +
  annotate("text", x=1.5, y=0.04, label=gemini_label_2, parse=TRUE, size=2.5, color="grey40", hjust=0) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=c(1, 2, 5, 10, 20, 50), trans="log2") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim=ylim_sat) +
  labs(title = "a",
       x="RLM depth (turns)", y="Mean concept recall (%)") +
  theme_nmi() + theme(plot.margin=margin(2, 2, 2, 2),
                       plot.title.position = "plot")

# ── Panel b: Local models (8 models, logistic fits, same y-axis) ──
# Separate models by logistic fit quality (R² >= 0.8 = good fit)
R2_THRESHOLD <- 0.8
local_curves <- tibble()
local_points <- tibble()
for (i in seq_len(nrow(local_spec))) {
  mk <- local_spec$model_key[i]
  dn <- local_spec$display[i]
  col <- local_spec$color[i]
  mdata <- v49_data |> filter(model == mk)
  if (nrow(mdata) == 0) next
  model_r2 <- if (!is.null(logistic_params[[dn]])) logistic_params[[dn]]$r2 else 0
  local_points <- bind_rows(local_points, mdata |> mutate(display=dn, color=col,
                                                            params_B=local_spec$params_B[i],
                                                            good_fit=model_r2 >= R2_THRESHOLD))
  if (!is.null(logistic_params[[dn]])) {
    local_curves <- bind_rows(local_curves, logistic_params[[dn]]$curve |>
                                mutate(display=dn, color=col, params_B=local_spec$params_B[i],
                                       good_fit=model_r2 >= R2_THRESHOLD))
  }
}

# Compute L vs model size Spearman correlation over ALL open-weight models
# (regardless of fit quality). Filtering on fit quality would be a post-hoc
# selection that biases against the "no parameter-count pattern" finding.
L_vals <- tibble(
  display = names(logistic_params),
  L = map_dbl(logistic_params, ~.x$L),
  r2 = map_dbl(logistic_params, ~.x$r2)
) |> inner_join(all_models_spec |> select(display, params_B), by = "display") |>
  filter(!is.na(params_B))

if (nrow(L_vals) >= 3) {
  L_cor <- cor.test(L_vals$params_B, L_vals$L, method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)
  L_subtitle <- sprintf("L does not track model size (\u03c1 = %.2f, p = %.2f, n = %d)",
                         L_cor$estimate, L_cor$p.value, nrow(L_vals))
} else {
  L_subtitle <- "Ceiling L variable across models"
}

# Models with poor logistic fit (R² < 0.8) shown with dashed lines
p3b <- ggplot() +
  annotate("rect", xmin=0.5, xmax=10, ymin=-Inf, ymax=Inf, fill=col_good, alpha=0.04) +
  # Curves: solid for good fit, dashed for poor fit
  geom_line(data=local_curves |> filter(good_fit),
            aes(x=depth, y=cr, color=display), linewidth=0.8) +
  geom_line(data=local_curves |> filter(!good_fit),
            aes(x=depth, y=cr, color=display), linewidth=0.6, linetype="dashed") +
  # Points
  geom_errorbar(data=local_points, aes(x=depth, ymin=ci_lower, ymax=ci_upper, color=display),
                width=0.5, alpha=0.4) +
  geom_point(data=local_points |> filter(good_fit),
             aes(x=depth, y=mean_cr, color=display), size=2, shape=21, fill="white", stroke=0.8) +
  geom_point(data=local_points |> filter(!good_fit),
             aes(x=depth, y=mean_cr, color=display), size=1.5, shape=4, stroke=0.6) +
  # Annotation
  annotate("text", x=50, y=0.03, label="\u2190 dashed: R\u00b2 < 0.8",
           size=2, color="grey50", fontface="italic", hjust=1) +
  scale_color_manual(values=setNames(local_spec$color, local_spec$display), name=NULL) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=c(1, 2, 5, 10, 20, 50), trans="log2") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim=ylim_sat) +
  labs(title = "b",
       x="RLM depth (turns)", y=NULL) +
  theme_nmi() +
  theme(legend.position=c(0.12, 0.78), legend.key.size=unit(2.5, "mm"),
        legend.text=element_text(size=4.5), legend.background=element_blank(),
        plot.margin=margin(2, 2, 2, 2),
        plot.title.position = "plot")

# ── Panel c: R² heatmap ──
r2_df <- as_tibble(r2_matrix, rownames="model") |>
  pivot_longer(cols=all_of(fit_types), names_to="fit_type", values_to="r2") |>
  mutate(fit_type=factor(fit_type, levels=fit_types),
         model=factor(model, levels=rev(all_models_spec$display)))

p3c <- ggplot(r2_df, aes(x=fit_type, y=model, fill=r2)) +
  geom_tile(color="white", linewidth=0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label=ifelse(is.na(r2), "-", sprintf("%.3f", r2))), size=2.5, fontface="bold") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low="#D55E00", mid="#F0E442", high="#009E73", midpoint=0.7, na.value="grey90", limits=c(0,1), name="R\u00b2") +
  labs(title = "c", x=NULL, y=NULL) +
  theme_nmi() +
  theme(axis.text.x=element_text(angle=45, hjust=1),
        axis.text.y=element_text(size=7),
        legend.position="none",
        plot.margin=margin(10, 2, 2, 2),
        plot.title.position = "plot")

# ── Panel b: Strategy crossover by corpus size (all from V4-5 clean) ──
if (has_v45) {
  scaling_data <- v45_clean |>
    filter(answerable == TRUE, noise_ratio == 0) |>
    group_by(config, config_label, core_size) |>
    summarise(
      mean_corr = mean(correctness, na.rm = TRUE),
      pass_rate = mean(pass, na.rm = TRUE),
      n = n(), .groups = "drop"
    ) |>
    filter(n >= 20)  # exclude unreliable small-N conditions (e.g. multi_hop s50 N=6)
} else {
  scaling_data <- ans |>
    group_by(config, config_label) |>
    summarise(
      mean_corr = mean(correctness, na.rm = TRUE),
      pass_rate = mean(passed, na.rm = TRUE),
      n = n(), .groups = "drop"
    ) |>
    mutate(core_size = 20L)
}

p3b_scaling <- scaling_data |>
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(core_size), y = pass_rate,
             color = config, group = config)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = config_palette, labels = config_labels, name = NULL) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = pct100, limits = c(0, 1.05)) +
  labs(title = "d",
       x = "Corpus size (papers)", y = "Pass rate (%)") +
  theme_nmi() +
  theme(legend.position = c(0.5, 0.12), legend.direction = "horizontal",
        legend.key.size = unit(2.5, "mm"),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 5),
        legend.background = element_rect(fill = alpha("white", 0.85), color = NA),
        plot.margin = margin(10, 2, 2, 2),
        plot.title.position = "plot")

# ── Panels e+f: Noise robustness (answerable pass rate + OOD refusal) ──
# Combined into a single faceted plot with shared noise legend
if (has_v45) {
  noise_pass <- v45_clean |>
    filter(answerable == TRUE, !is.na(correctness), core_size == 20) |>
    mutate(noise_label = factor(noise_ratio, levels = c(0, 1, 3),
                                labels = c("0\u00d7", "1\u00d7", "3\u00d7"))) |>
    group_by(config_label, config, noise_label) |>
    summarise(value = mean(pass, na.rm = TRUE), n = n(), .groups = "drop") |>
    mutate(metric = "e  Pass rate (answerable)")

  v45_ood_clean <- v45_clean |>
    filter(answerable == FALSE, !is.na(refusal_accuracy))

  noise_refusal <- if (nrow(v45_ood_clean) > 0) {
    v45_ood_clean |>
      mutate(noise_label = factor(noise_ratio, levels = c(0, 1, 3),
                                  labels = c("0\u00d7", "1\u00d7", "3\u00d7"))) |>
      group_by(config_label, config, noise_label) |>
      summarise(value = mean(refusal_accuracy >= 3, na.rm = TRUE),
                n = n(), .groups = "drop") |>
      filter(n >= 5) |>
      mutate(metric = "f  Refusal rate (OOD)")
  } else { tibble() }

  noise_combined <- bind_rows(noise_pass, noise_refusal) |>
    mutate(metric = factor(metric, levels = c("e  Pass rate (answerable)", "f  Refusal rate (OOD)")))

  p3ef <- noise_combined |>
    ggplot(aes(x = config_label, y = value, fill = noise_label)) +
    geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.7, alpha = 0.85) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c(col_good, "#fd7e14", col_bad), name = "Noise") +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = pct100, limits = c(0, 1.05),
                       expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05))) +
    facet_wrap(~metric, ncol = 2) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = "Rate (%)") +
    theme_nmi() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
          strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11, hjust = 0),
          strip.background = element_blank(),
          strip.placement = "outside",
          legend.position = "bottom",
          legend.direction = "horizontal",
          legend.key.size = unit(3, "mm"), legend.text = element_text(size = 7),
          plot.margin = margin(10, 4, 2, 4),
          plot.title.position = "plot")
} else {
  p3ef <- ggplot() + annotate("text", x = 0.5, y = 0.5, label = "V4-5 data not available") + theme_void()
}

# ── Rename scaling panel ──
p3d <- p3b_scaling

# ── Compose: all rows share 1:2 width ratio for consistent alignment ──
# Row 2 built with gridExtra to bypass patchwork's axis alignment
# (prevents panel c's rotated labels from forcing a gap under panel d)
library(grid)
library(gridExtra)
row2_grid <- arrangeGrob(
  ggplotGrob(p3c), ggplotGrob(p3d),
  ncol = 2, widths = unit(c(1, 2), "null")
)

fig3 <- (free(p3a) + p3b + plot_layout(widths = c(1, 2))) /
        wrap_elements(full = row2_grid) /
        free(p3ef) +
  plot_layout(heights = c(1.1, 0.9, 1)) +
  plot_annotation(
    theme = theme_nmi() + theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold", margin = margin(b = 4)))
  )

fig3
save_figure(fig3, "fig3_scaling_behavior", w = 180, h = 230)
```

---

# Figure 4 — Pipeline Ablation {.tabset}

Four additive pipeline configurations evaluated on the same 20-paper corpus
(138 questions, 552 total evaluations). Data from V4-6 experiment.

```{r fig5-ablation-build, fig.width=10, fig.height=9, eval=has_v46}

# ── Shared config ──
ablation_order <- c("raw_gemini", "naive_rag", "rlm_10", "full_orchestrator")
ablation_labels <- c(
  raw_gemini = "Baseline", naive_rag = "Naive RAG",
  rlm_10 = "RLM-10", full_orchestrator = "Full Orchestrator"
)
ablation_palette <- c(
  raw_gemini = "#fd7e14", naive_rag = col_info,
  rlm_10 = col_good, full_orchestrator = "#6f42c1"
)

# Shared theme for all 4 panels — identical margins so titles align
fig4_theme <- theme_nmi() +
  theme(plot.margin = margin(2, 4, 2, 4),
        plot.title.position = "plot")

# ── Data prep ──
abl_data <- v46_pass |>
  filter(config %in% ablation_order) |>
  mutate(config = factor(config, levels = ablation_order),
         pass_frac = pass_rate / 100)

scat_data <- v46_summary |>
  filter(!is.na(pass_rate), !is.na(refusal_rate)) |>
  mutate(pass_frac = pass_rate / 100, refusal_frac = refusal_rate / 100,
         label_clean = ablation_labels[config])

bar_data <- v46_summary |>
  filter(config %in% ablation_order) |>
  mutate(config = factor(config, levels = ablation_order))

# ── Panel a: Pass rate ──
p5a <- ggplot(abl_data, aes(x = config, y = pass_frac, fill = config)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", pass_rate)), vjust = -0.5, size = 2.5, fontface = "bold") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.8, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.25, color = "grey50") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = ablation_palette, guide = "none") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = ablation_labels) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = pct100, limits = c(0, 1.05)) +
  labs(title = "a",
       x = NULL, y = "Pass rate (%)") +
  fig4_theme +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1, size = 6))

# ── Panel b: Safety scatter ──
p5b <- ggplot(scat_data, aes(x = pass_frac, y = refusal_frac, color = config)) +
  geom_point(size = 4) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(aes(label = label_clean), size = 2.5, show.legend = FALSE,
                            color = "grey30", max.overlaps = 10, nudge_y = 0.05) +
  scale_color_manual(values = ablation_palette, guide = "none") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = pct100, limits = c(0, 1.05)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = pct100, limits = c(-0.05, 1.05)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.25, color = "grey50") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.25, color = "grey50") +
  annotate("text", x = 0.53, y = -0.04, label = "Accurate but uncritical",
           size = 1.8, color = col_bad, fontface = "italic", hjust = 0) +
  labs(title = "b",
       x = "Pass rate, answerable (%)", y = "Refusal rate, unanswerable (%)") +
  fig4_theme

# ── Panel c: Mean correctness ──
p5c <- ggplot(bar_data, aes(x = config, y = mean_correctness, fill = config)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", mean_correctness)), vjust = -0.5, size = 2.5, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = ablation_palette, guide = "none") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = ablation_labels) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 4.8), breaks = 0:5) +
  labs(title = "c",
       x = NULL, y = "Mean correctness (0-5)") +
  fig4_theme +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1, size = 6))

# ── Panel d: Component accuracy heatmap (V4-7) ──
load_v47_fig4 <- function(component) {
  files <- list.files(results_dir, pattern = paste0("exp07_bench_", component, "_.*\\.json"),
                      full.names = TRUE)
  files <- files[!grepl("dataset|_results_", basename(files))]
  all_results <- list()
  for (f in files) {
    d <- tryCatch(fix_json(f), error = function(e) NULL)
    if (is.null(d) || is.null(d$results) || length(d$results) == 0) next
    df <- tryCatch(as_tibble(d$results), error = function(e) NULL)
    if (is.null(df) || nrow(df) == 0) next
    all_results[[length(all_results) + 1]] <- df
  }
  if (length(all_results) == 0) return(tibble())
  bind_rows(all_results) |>
    mutate(model_short = canonical_model_name(model_key),
           component = component)
}

v47_fig4 <- map(c("supervisor", "distiller", "analyzer", "synthesizer", "planner"),
                load_v47_fig4) |> bind_rows()
benchmarks_fig4 <- c("supervisor", "distiller", "analyzer", "synthesizer", "planner")

met_fig4_rows <- list()
for (comp in benchmarks_fig4) {
  df <- v47_fig4 |> filter(component == comp)
  if (nrow(df) == 0) next
  for (mk in unique(df$model_key)) {
    sub <- df |> filter(model_key == mk, is.na(error) | error == "" | !grepl("404", error))
    n <- nrow(sub)
    if (n == 0) next
    acc <- switch(comp,
      supervisor = {
        qs <- sub$quality_score[!is.na(sub$quality_score) & sub$score_valid]
        jc <- sub$`_judge_correctness`[!is.na(sub$quality_score) & sub$score_valid]
        if (length(qs) > 10 && length(jc) == length(qs))
          100 * abs(cor(qs, jc, method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs"))
        else NA_real_
      },
      distiller   = 100 * mean(sub$number_retention, na.rm = TRUE),
      analyzer    = 100 * sum(sub$decision_correct, na.rm = TRUE) / n,
      synthesizer = {
        sc <- sub$judge_scores.correctness
        if (!is.null(sc) && sum(!is.na(sc)) > 0) 100 * mean(sc, na.rm = TRUE) / 5 else NA_real_
      },
      planner     = 100 * sum(sub$tool_correct, na.rm = TRUE) / n
    )
    met_fig4_rows[[length(met_fig4_rows) + 1]] <- tibble(
      model = sub$model_short[1], component = comp, accuracy = acc, n = n)
  }
}

met_fig4 <- bind_rows(met_fig4_rows) |>
  mutate(component = factor(component, levels = benchmarks_fig4),
         comp_label = recode(component, supervisor = "Supervisor", distiller = "Distiller",
                             analyzer = "Analyzer", synthesizer = "Synthesizer", planner = "Planner")) |>
  filter(!grepl("qwen3\\.5.*\\+think", model)) |>
  group_by(model) |> filter(sum(!is.na(accuracy)) >= 4) |> ungroup()

model_ord <- met_fig4 |> filter(!is.na(accuracy)) |>
  group_by(model) |> summarise(ma = mean(accuracy)) |> arrange(desc(ma)) |> pull(model)
met_fig4 <- met_fig4 |> mutate(model = factor(model, levels = rev(model_ord)))

heat_fig4 <- met_fig4 |>
  mutate(label = ifelse(is.na(accuracy), "\u2014", sprintf("%.0f%%", accuracy)),
         fill_val = ifelse(is.na(accuracy), 50, accuracy),
         text_col = "black")  # always black per author preference

p5d <- ggplot(heat_fig4, aes(x = comp_label, y = model, fill = fill_val)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_text(aes(label = label, color = text_col), size = 2.2, fontface = "bold", show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = col_bad, mid = col_mid, high = col_good,
                       midpoint = 60, limits = c(0, 100), name = "%") +
  scale_color_identity() +
  labs(title = "d",
       x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  fig4_theme +
  theme(legend.key.height = unit(0.25, "cm"), legend.key.width = unit(0.5, "cm"),
        legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))

# ── Compose with patchwork: per-row alignment ──
# Each row is built independently with `(left | right) + plot_layout(widths)`,
# then wrapped as an opaque element so cross-row alignment is NOT enforced.
# Why: the heatmap in panel d has long y-axis labels (model names like
# "nemotron-3-nano:30b") that would otherwise inflate the left margin of
# panel b above it, leaving an ugly gap between panel b's y-axis title and
# its short tick labels (0, 25, 50, 75, 100). Per-row layout keeps panel b's
# left margin tight to its own content, and panels a vs c (with similar
# y-axis label widths) still appear visually aligned even without strict
# cross-row enforcement.
top_row <- (p5a | p5b) + plot_layout(widths = c(1, 1.8))
bot_row <- (p5c | p5d) + plot_layout(widths = c(1, 1.8))

fig5_ablation <- (wrap_elements(full = top_row) /
                  wrap_elements(full = bot_row)) +
  plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1.1)) +
  plot_annotation(
    theme = theme_nmi() + theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold", margin = margin(b = 4)))
  )

fig5_ablation
save_figure(fig5_ablation, "fig4_pipeline_ablation", w = 180, h = 230)
```

---

# Figure 5 — Triple-Triangulation Evaluation {.tabset}

```{r fig6-triangulation-build, fig.width=10, fig.height=10}

# ── Panel a: Judge faithfulness score distribution ──
faith_scores <- det_ans |>
  filter(!is.na(judge_faithfulness)) |>
  pull(judge_faithfulness)

faith_dist <- tibble(score = faith_scores) |>
  count(score) |>
  complete(score = 0:5, fill = list(n = 0)) |>
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n))

p6a <- faith_dist |>
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(score), y = pct, fill = ifelse(score == 5, "ceiling", "other"))) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(pct > 0, sprintf("%.1f%%", pct * 100), "")),
            vjust = -0.3, size = 2.5, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("ceiling" = col_bad, "other" = col_muted), guide = "none") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = pct100, limits = c(0, 0.9)) +
  labs(title = "a",
       x = "Judge faithfulness score", y = "Fraction of answers (%)") +
  theme_nmi()

# ── Panel b: Judge faithfulness vs MiniCheck precision (scatter) ──
if (has_minicheck) {
  mc_box <- mc |>
    filter(!is.na(judge_faith) & !is.na(mc_precision)) |>
    mutate(judge_bin = factor(as.integer(judge_faith)))

  rho_faith_mc <- cor(mc_box$judge_faith, mc_box$mc_precision, method = "spearman")

  # Compute per-bin stats for annotation
  bin_stats <- mc_box |>
    group_by(judge_bin) |>
    summarise(med = median(mc_precision), mean_val = mean(mc_precision),
              n = n(), .groups = "drop")

  p6b <- mc_box |>
    ggplot(aes(x = judge_bin, y = mc_precision)) +
    geom_boxplot(fill = col_muted, alpha = 0.3, outlier.size = 0.8, outlier.alpha = 0.4) +
    geom_text(data = bin_stats, aes(x = judge_bin, y = 1.05,
              label = sprintf("n=%d", n)), size = 2, color = "grey50") +
    geom_text(data = bin_stats, aes(x = judge_bin, y = med,
              label = sprintf("%.0f%%", mean_val * 100)),
              size = 2.5, fontface = "bold", color = col_bad, vjust = -0.5) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1.1), labels = pct100, breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.25)) +
    labs(title = "b",
         x = "Judge faithfulness score", y = "MiniCheck precision (%)") +
    theme_nmi()
} else {
  p6b <- ggplot() + annotate("text", x = 0.5, y = 0.5, label = "MiniCheck data not available") + theme_void()
  rho_faith_mc <- NA
}

# ── Panel c: Cross-method correlation by dimension ──
dims <- c("correctness", "completeness", "faithfulness", "citation_quality")
dim_labels_v <- c("Correctness", "Completeness", "Faithfulness", "Citation\nQuality")

agreement_stats <- map_dfr(seq_along(dims), function(i) {
  dim <- dims[i]
  d_vals <- det_ans[[paste0("det_", dim)]]
  j_vals <- det_ans[[paste0("judge_", dim)]]
  valid <- !is.na(d_vals) & !is.na(j_vals)
  d <- d_vals[valid]; j <- j_vals[valid]
  tibble(
    Dimension = dim_labels_v[i],
    `Spearman rho` = if (length(d) > 2 & sd(d) > 0 & sd(j) > 0)
      cor(d, j, method = "spearman") else NA_real_
  )
})

p6c <- agreement_stats |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Dimension, y = `Spearman rho`,
             fill = ifelse(`Spearman rho` > 0.3, "moderate", "weak"))) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(`Spearman rho`, 3)), vjust = -0.5, size = 3, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("moderate" = col_info, "weak" = col_bad), guide = "none") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.8)) +
  labs(title = "c",
       x = NULL, y = expression(paste("Spearman ", rho))) +
  theme_nmi() + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5))

# ── Panel d: Bump chart — overall strategy rankings across three methods ──
# Use pre-computed rankings from analysis_paper_validation.json (source of W = 0.911)
# In validation: rank 1 = worst, rank 6 = best. Invert for display: rank 1 = best.
if (has_minicheck) {
  val_data <- safe_load(file.path(results_dir, "analysis_paper_validation.json"))
  kw <- val_data$kendalls_w$config_details

  rank_data <- map_dfr(names(kw), function(cfg) {
    info <- kw[[cfg]]
    lbl <- config_labels[cfg]
    tibble(
      Strategy = factor(lbl, levels = config_labels),
      Method = c("MiniCheck", "Deterministic", "Judge"),
      Rank = 7 - c(info$mc_f1_rank, info$det_rank, info$judge_rank)  # invert: 1=best
    )
  }) |>
    mutate(Method = factor(Method, levels = c("MiniCheck", "Deterministic", "Judge")))

  p6d <- rank_data |>
    ggplot(aes(x = Method, y = Rank, group = Strategy, color = Strategy)) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 0.9, alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_point(size = 3.5) +
    scale_y_reverse(breaks = 1:6, labels = paste0("#", 1:6)) +
    scale_color_manual(values = setNames(config_palette, config_labels), name = NULL) +
    labs(title = "d",
         subtitle = "Kendall\u2019s W = 0.91, P = 0.018",
         x = NULL, y = "Rank (1 = best)") +
    theme_nmi() +
    theme(panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
          legend.position = "bottom",
          legend.key.size = unit(3, "mm"),
          legend.text = element_text(size = 6))
} else {
  p6d <- ggplot() + annotate("text", x = 0.5, y = 0.5, label = "MiniCheck data not available") + theme_void()
}

# ── Compose (2x2 grid) ──
fig6_tri <- (p6a | p6b) / (p6c | p6d) +
  plot_annotation(
    theme = theme_nmi() + theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold", margin = margin(b = 4)))
  )

fig6_tri
save_figure(fig6_tri, "fig5_triangulation", w = 180, h = 200)
```

---

---

# Extended Data Figure 6 — Per-Dimension Ranking Concordance {.tabset}

Shows that strategy ranking convergence (W = 0.911 overall) holds for
correctness and completeness but breaks down for faithfulness and citation quality,
visually confirming the dimension-specific failure from Fig 6c.

```{r ed-fig10-dimension-rankings, fig.width=10, fig.height=8, eval=has_minicheck}

# Compute rankings per dimension across three methods
dims_for_rank <- c("correctness", "completeness", "faithfulness", "citation_quality")
dim_titles <- c("Correctness", "Completeness", "Faithfulness", "Citation Quality")

# MiniCheck only measures grounding — include it only for faithfulness and citation quality
dims_with_mc <- c("faithfulness", "citation_quality")

dim_rank_data <- map_dfr(seq_along(dims_for_rank), function(i) {
  dim <- dims_for_rank[i]
  include_mc <- dim %in% dims_with_mc

  # Deterministic and Judge scores from det_ans
  det_judge <- det_ans |>
    group_by(config_label) |>
    summarise(
      det_score = mean(.data[[paste0("det_", dim)]], na.rm = TRUE),
      judge_score = mean(.data[[paste0("judge_", dim)]], na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    )

  if (include_mc) {
    mc_dim <- mc |>
      group_by(config_label) |>
      summarise(mc_score = mean(mc_precision, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

    combined <- det_judge |>
      left_join(mc_dim, by = "config_label") |>
      transmute(
        Strategy = config_label, Dimension = dim_titles[i],
        MiniCheck = mc_score, Deterministic = det_score, Judge = judge_score
      ) |>
      pivot_longer(c(MiniCheck, Deterministic, Judge),
                   names_to = "Method", values_to = "score")
  } else {
    combined <- det_judge |>
      transmute(
        Strategy = config_label, Dimension = dim_titles[i],
        Deterministic = det_score, Judge = judge_score
      ) |>
      pivot_longer(c(Deterministic, Judge),
                   names_to = "Method", values_to = "score")
  }

  combined |>
    group_by(Method) |>
    mutate(Rank = rank(-score, ties.method = "first")) |>
    ungroup() |>
    mutate(Method = factor(Method, levels = c("MiniCheck", "Deterministic", "Judge")))
})

ed_fig10 <- dim_rank_data |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Method, y = Rank, group = Strategy, color = Strategy)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5) +
  scale_y_reverse(breaks = 1:6, labels = paste0("#", 1:6)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(config_palette, config_labels), name = NULL) +
  facet_wrap(~ Dimension, nrow = 2) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Rank (1 = best)") +
  theme_nmi() +
  theme(panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.key.size = unit(3, "mm"),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10))

ed_fig10
save_figure(ed_fig10, "ed_fig6_dimension_rankings", w = 180, h = 160)
```

---

# Figure 6 — Code Generation {.tabset}

```{r fig6-build, fig.width=10, fig.height=10, eval=has_v48 && has_v48_models}
# --- Build per-model × strategy data from V4-8 results ---
all_strategies <- c("free_form", "free_form_n3_best", "free_form_n3_combined",
                    "coder_v2_n1", "coder_v2_n1_cell3_best", "coder_v2_n1_cell3_combined",
                    "coder_v2_n3", "introspect_then_code", "error_recovery_introspect",
                    "thinker_coder_split", "introspect_with_recovery")

v48_config_data <- map_dfr(v48_models, function(d) {
  think_mode <- d$think %||% "off"
  # Normalise think_mode: True -> on, False/off/NULL -> off
  if (think_mode %in% c("True", "true", "TRUE")) think_mode <- "on"
  if (think_mode %in% c("False", "false", "FALSE", "")) think_mode <- "off"
  raw_name <- d$gen_model %||% "unknown"
  base_label <- canonical_model_name(raw_name)
  label <- if (think_mode != "off") paste0(base_label, " [think:", think_mode, "]") else base_label
  is_gemini <- grepl("gemini", raw_name, ignore.case = TRUE)
  # Build rows for every strategy; mark missing ones as NA (not 0)
  map_dfr(all_strategies, function(cfg) {
    s <- d$summary[[cfg]]
    tibble(
      model      = label,
      config     = cfg,
      pass_rate  = if (!is.null(s)) (s$pass_rate %||% NA_real_) else NA_real_,
      n          = if (!is.null(s)) (s$n %||% NA_integer_) else NA_integer_,
      is_gemini  = is_gemini
    )
  })
})

# Clean strategy labels (reused in panels a & c)
strategy_label <- function(x) {
  x |>
    str_replace("coder_v2_n1_cell3_best", "Structured Best-of-3") |>
    str_replace("coder_v2_n1_cell3_combined", "Structured Combined") |>
    str_replace("coder_v2_n1$", "Structured") |>
    str_replace("coder_v2_n3", "Structured \u00d73") |>
    str_replace("free_form_n3_best", "Free-form Best-of-3") |>
    str_replace("free_form_n3_combined", "Free-form Combined") |>
    str_replace("free_form", "Free-form") |>
    str_replace("error_recovery_introspect", "Error Recovery Introspect") |>
    str_replace("introspect_then_code", "Introspect Then Code") |>
    str_replace("introspect_with_recovery", "Introspect With Recovery") |>
    str_replace("thinker_coder_split", "Thinker-Coder Split")
}

# Model ordering: mean pass rate (descending), computed only on non-NA cells
model_order <- v48_config_data |>
  filter(!is.na(pass_rate)) |>
  group_by(model) |>
  summarise(mean_pr = mean(pass_rate), .groups = "drop") |>
  arrange(mean_pr) |>
  pull(model)

# Strategy order: grouped by family (free-form, introspection, structured)
strategy_family_order <- c(
  "Free-form", "Free-form Best-of-3", "Free-form Combined",
  "Error Recovery Introspect", "Introspect Then Code",
  "Introspect With Recovery", "Thinker-Coder Split",
  "Structured", "Structured Best-of-3", "Structured Combined", "Structured \u00d73"
)

v48_config_data <- v48_config_data |>
  mutate(model = factor(model, levels = model_order),
         config_label = factor(strategy_label(config), levels = strategy_family_order))

# ── Panel a: Pass rate heatmap ─────────────────────────────────────────
p6a <- v48_config_data |>
  ggplot(aes(x = config_label, y = model, fill = pass_rate / 100)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(is.na(pass_rate), "", sprintf("%.0f%%", pass_rate))),
            size = 2) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = col_bad, mid = col_mid, high = col_good,
                       midpoint = 0.5, limits = c(0, 1), name = "Pass rate (%)",
                       labels = pct100, na.value = "grey85") +
  labs(title = "c",
       x = "Strategy", y = NULL) +
  theme_nmi() + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
                      legend.position = "bottom",
                      legend.margin = margin(t = 14),
                      legend.key.height = unit(0.3, "cm"),
                      legend.key.width = unit(0.8, "cm"))

# ── Panel b: Context window effect (V2-8 vs V4-8) ─────────────────────
v48_df <- map_dfr(names(v48_comp$models), function(model_key) {
  m <- v48_comp$models[[model_key]]
  tibble(
    model     = model_key,
    ctx_2k    = m$v2_8_pass_rate %||% NA_real_,
    ctx_24k   = m$v4_8_pass_rate %||% NA_real_,
    is_gemini = m$is_gemini %||% FALSE
  )
}) |> filter(!is.na(ctx_24k), !is.na(ctx_2k), !is_gemini)

# Prepare wide data for right-aligned labels
v48_wide <- v48_df |>
  mutate(model_short = canonical_model_name(model),
         label = sprintf("%.0f%% > %.0f%%", ctx_2k, ctx_24k))

v48_long <- v48_df |>
  pivot_longer(cols = c(ctx_2k, ctx_24k), names_to = "version", values_to = "pass_rate") |>
  mutate(
    version = ifelse(version == "ctx_2k",
                     "Default Context (2K)", "Expanded Context (24K)"),
    version = factor(version, levels = c("Default Context (2K)", "Expanded Context (24K)")),
    model_short = canonical_model_name(model)
  )

# Order by expanded context pass rate
model_ctx_order <- v48_wide |> arrange(ctx_24k) |> pull(model_short)
v48_long <- v48_long |> mutate(model_short = factor(model_short, levels = model_ctx_order))
v48_wide <- v48_wide |> mutate(model_short = factor(model_short, levels = model_ctx_order))

p6b <- ggplot(v48_long, aes(x = model_short, y = pass_rate, fill = version)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge2(width = 0.7, reverse = TRUE), width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(data = v48_wide, aes(x = model_short, y = 103, label = label, fill = NULL),
            size = 2.0, hjust = 0, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Default Context (2K)" = col_bad,
                                "Expanded Context (24K)" = col_good)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 140), breaks = seq(0, 100, 25)) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "b",
       x = NULL, y = "Pass rate (%)", fill = NULL) +
  theme_nmi() + theme(legend.position = "bottom",
                      legend.direction = "horizontal",
                      legend.key.size = unit(3, "mm"),
                      legend.text = element_text(size = 5),
                      legend.margin = margin(t = 12),
                      plot.margin = margin(2, 2, -2, 2))

# ── Panel c: Strategy effectiveness (mean across models) ──────────────
strategy_data <- v48_config_data |>
  filter(!is.na(pass_rate)) |>
  group_by(config, config_label) |>
  summarise(mean_pass = mean(pass_rate, na.rm = TRUE),
            n_models = n(), .groups = "drop")

p6c <- if (nrow(strategy_data) > 0) {
  strategy_data |>
    ggplot(aes(x = reorder(config_label, mean_pass), y = mean_pass)) +
    geom_col(width = 0.6, fill = col_info) +
    geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", mean_pass)),
              hjust = -0.1, size = 2.5, fontface = "bold") +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 105)) +
    coord_flip() +
    labs(title = "a",
         x = NULL, y = "Mean pass rate (%)") +
    theme_nmi()
} else {
  ggplot() +
    annotate("text", x = 0.5, y = 0.5,
             label = "Strategy config data not available") +
    theme_void()
}

# ── Compose Figure 6 using grid for alignment ────────────────────────
library(grid)
library(gridExtra)

g6a <- ggplotGrob(p6c)  # strategy = panel a
g6b <- ggplotGrob(p6a)  # heatmap = panel b
g6c <- ggplotGrob(p6b)  # context window = panel c

top_row_fig6 <- cbind(g6a, g6c, size = "first")

fig6_grid <- arrangeGrob(
  top_row_fig6,
  nullGrob(),
  g6b,
  nrow = 3,
  heights = unit(c(1, 0.05, 1.3), "null")
)

fig6 <- wrap_elements(full = fig6_grid) +
  plot_annotation(
    theme = theme_nmi() + theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold", margin = margin(b = 4)))
  )

fig6
save_figure(fig6, "fig6_code_generation", w = 180, h = 220)
```

---

# Extended Data Figure 1 — Retrieval Pipeline Optimization {.tabset}

```{r ed-fig1-retrieval-build, fig.width=10, fig.height=10, eval=!is.null(v41) && !is.null(v42) && !is.null(v43a) && !is.null(v43b)}
# Panel a: V4-1 Embedding — with model labels on the right side and distinct line types
v41_df <- v41$results |>
  as_tibble() |>
  mutate(model_short = model |> str_remove("sentence-transformers/") |> str_remove("_base$"))

v41_overall <- v41_df |>
  select(model_short, corpus_size, starts_with("overall.")) |>
  rename_with(~str_remove(.x, "overall\\."), starts_with("overall."))

ed1a <- v41_overall |>
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(corpus_size), y = MRR, color = model_short,
             group = model_short, linetype = model_short)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = model_palette[1:4]) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("solid", "dashed", "dotted", "dotdash")) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0.3, 1.05)) +
  labs(title = "a",
       x = "Corpus size (papers)", y = "MRR",
       color = NULL, linetype = NULL) +
  theme_nmi() +
  theme(legend.position = "inside",
        legend.position.inside = c(0.95, 0.05),
        legend.justification = c(1, 0),
        legend.background = element_rect(fill = alpha("white", 0.85), color = NA),
        legend.key.width = unit(8, "mm"),
        legend.key.height = unit(3, "mm"),
        plot.margin = margin(4, 2, 4, 2)) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(ncol = 2),
         linetype = guide_legend(ncol = 2))

# Panel b: V4-2 Search Strategy
v42_df <- v42$results |>
  as_tibble() |>
  mutate(config_short = config |> str_remove("^exp_v4_") |> str_remove("_30$"))

v42_overall <- v42_df |>
  select(config_short, corpus_size, starts_with("overall.")) |>
  rename_with(~str_remove(.x, "overall\\."), starts_with("overall."))

ed1b <- v42_overall |>
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(corpus_size), y = MRR, color = config_short, group = config_short)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = model_palette[1:7]) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0.8, 1.02)) +
  labs(title = "b",
       x = "Corpus size (papers)", y = "MRR", color = NULL) +
  theme_nmi() +
  theme(legend.position = "inside",
        legend.position.inside = c(0.95, 0.05),
        legend.justification = c(1, 0),
        legend.background = element_rect(fill = alpha("white", 0.85), color = NA),
        legend.key.width = unit(6, "mm"),
        legend.key.height = unit(3, "mm"),
        plot.margin = margin(4, 2, 4, 2)) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(ncol = 2))

# Panel c: V4-3a Chunking — horizontal bar plot (clearer than tight vertical)
chunk_df <- v43a$results |>
  as_tibble() |>
  mutate(config_short = config |> str_remove("^exp_v4_") |> str_remove("_30$"))

chunk_summary <- chunk_df |>
  group_by(config_short) |>
  summarise(mean_mrr = mean(overall.MRR, na.rm = TRUE),
            sd_mrr = sd(overall.MRR, na.rm = TRUE),
            n = n(), .groups = "drop")

ed1c <- chunk_summary |>
  mutate(config_short = fct_reorder(config_short, mean_mrr)) |>
  ggplot(aes(y = config_short, x = mean_mrr)) +
  geom_point(color = col_info, size = 2.5) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = mean_mrr - sd_mrr, xmax = pmin(mean_mrr + sd_mrr, 1.05)), height = 0.3) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.3f", mean_mrr), x = 1.06), hjust = 0, size = 2.8) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0.5, 1.15)) +
  labs(title = "c",
       x = "MRR", y = NULL) +
  theme_nmi()

# Panel d: V4-3b Scalability — horizontal bar plot (clearer for tight data)
scale_df <- v43b$results |>
  as_tibble()

ed1d <- scale_df |>
  mutate(corpus_label = factor(corpus_size)) |>
  ggplot(aes(y = corpus_label, x = overall.MRR)) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = 0.8, xend = overall.MRR, yend = corpus_label),
               color = col_info, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(color = col_info, size = 3) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.3f", overall.MRR)), hjust = -0.3, size = 2.8, fontface = "bold") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.9, linetype = "dashed", color = col_muted, linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0.8, 1.08)) +
  labs(title = "d",
       x = "MRR", y = "Corpus size (papers)") +
  theme_nmi()

top_row <- (ed1a | ed1b)
bottom_row <- (ed1c | ed1d)

ed_fig1_retr <- (wrap_elements(full = top_row)) / bottom_row +
  plot_layout(heights = c(1.1, 1)) +
  plot_annotation(
    theme = theme_nmi() + theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold", margin = margin(b = 4)))
  )

ed_fig1_retr
save_figure(ed_fig1_retr, "ed_fig1_retrieval_optimisation", w = 180, h = 170)
```

# Extended Data Figure 4 — Component Benchmarks (V4-7)

**Purpose**: Show that orchestrator component performance varies dramatically by model. Six panels: (a) accuracy heatmap, (b-f) per-component latency vs accuracy vs token cost.

```{r ed-fig3-build, fig.width=7.09, fig.height=9.45}
# --- Load V4-7 data ---
load_v47_component_fig <- function(component) {
  files <- list.files(results_dir, pattern = paste0("exp07_bench_", component, "_.*\\.json"),
                      full.names = TRUE)
  files <- files[!grepl("dataset|_results_", basename(files))]
  all_results <- list()
  for (f in files) {
    d <- tryCatch(fix_json(f), error = function(e) NULL)
    if (is.null(d) || is.null(d$results) || length(d$results) == 0) next
    df <- tryCatch(as_tibble(d$results), error = function(e) NULL)
    if (is.null(df) || nrow(df) == 0) next
    all_results[[length(all_results) + 1]] <- df
  }
  if (length(all_results) == 0) return(tibble())
  bind_rows(all_results) |>
    mutate(
      model_short = canonical_model_name(model_key),
      component = component
    )
}

v47 <- list(
  supervisor  = load_v47_component_fig("supervisor"),
  distiller   = load_v47_component_fig("distiller"),
  analyzer    = load_v47_component_fig("analyzer"),
  synthesizer = load_v47_component_fig("synthesizer"),
  planner     = load_v47_component_fig("planner")
)

ds_sizes <- c(supervisor = 200, distiller = 97, analyzer = 345, synthesizer = 100, planner = 90)
benchmarks <- c("supervisor", "distiller", "analyzer", "synthesizer", "planner")

# --- Build accuracy + latency data ---
metrics_rows <- list()
for (comp in benchmarks) {
  df <- v47[[comp]]
  if (nrow(df) == 0) next
  for (mk in unique(df$model_key)) {
    sub <- df |> filter(model_key == mk, is.na(error) | error == "" | !grepl("404", error))
    n <- nrow(sub)
    if (n == 0) next
    acc <- switch(comp,
      supervisor  = {
        qs <- sub$quality_score[!is.na(sub$quality_score) & sub$score_valid]
        jc <- sub$`_judge_correctness`[!is.na(sub$quality_score) & sub$score_valid]
        if (length(qs) > 10 && length(jc) == length(qs))
          100 * abs(cor(qs, jc, method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs"))
        else NA_real_
      },
      distiller   = 100 * mean(sub$number_retention, na.rm = TRUE),
      analyzer    = 100 * sum(sub$decision_correct, na.rm = TRUE) / n,
      synthesizer = {
        sc <- sub$judge_scores.correctness
        if (!is.null(sc) && sum(!is.na(sc)) > 0) 100 * mean(sc, na.rm = TRUE) / 5 else NA_real_
      },
      planner     = 100 * sum(sub$tool_correct, na.rm = TRUE) / n
    )
    ms <- sub$model_short[1]
    lat <- mean(sub$latency_s[sub$latency_s > 0], na.rm = TRUE)
    tok <- mean(sub$tokens_out[sub$tokens_out > 0], na.rm = TRUE)
    metrics_rows[[length(metrics_rows) + 1]] <- tibble(
      model = ms, component = comp, accuracy = acc, latency = lat, tokens = tok, n = n
    )
  }
}

has_v47_data <- length(metrics_rows) > 0
if (!has_v47_data) {
  warning("No V4-7 data available — ED Figure 8 will be a placeholder")
}

if (has_v47_data) {
met_df <- bind_rows(metrics_rows) |>
  mutate(
    component = factor(component, levels = benchmarks),
    comp_label = recode(component,
      supervisor = "Supervisor", distiller = "Distiller",
      analyzer = "Analyzer", synthesizer = "Synthesizer", planner = "Planner")
  )

# Order models by mean accuracy
model_order <- met_df |>
  filter(!is.na(accuracy)) |>
  group_by(model) |> summarise(ma = mean(accuracy)) |>
  arrange(desc(ma)) |> pull(model)
met_df <- met_df |> mutate(model = factor(model, levels = rev(model_order)))

# --- Panel a: Accuracy heatmap ---
heat_df <- met_df |>
  mutate(
    label = ifelse(is.na(accuracy), "—", sprintf("%.0f%%", accuracy)),
    fill_val = ifelse(is.na(accuracy), 50, accuracy),
    text_col = "black"  # always black per author preference
  )

ed3a <- ggplot(heat_df, aes(x = comp_label, y = model, fill = fill_val)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_text(aes(label = label, color = text_col), size = 2.2, fontface = "bold", show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = col_bad, mid = col_mid, high = col_good,
                       midpoint = 60, limits = c(0, 100), name = "%") +
  scale_color_identity() +
  labs(title = "a", x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_nmi() + theme(legend.key.height = unit(0.3, "cm"), legend.key.width = unit(0.8, "cm"))

# --- Panel b: Latency heatmap ---
lat_df <- met_df |>
  mutate(
    label = ifelse(is.na(latency) | latency <= 0, "\u2014", sprintf("%.0fs", latency)),
    fill_val = ifelse(is.na(latency) | latency <= 0, NA_real_, log10(latency)),
    text_col = "black"  # always black per author preference
  )

ed3b <- ggplot(lat_df, aes(x = comp_label, y = model, fill = fill_val)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_text(aes(label = label, color = text_col), size = 2.2, fontface = "bold", show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = col_good, mid = col_mid, high = col_bad,
                       midpoint = 1.2, na.value = "grey85",
                       breaks = c(0.7, 1.0, 1.3, 1.6, 1.9, 2.2),
                       labels = c("5s", "10s", "20s", "40s", "80s", "160s"),
                       name = "Latency (s)") +
  scale_color_identity() +
  labs(title = "b", x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_nmi() + theme(legend.key.height = unit(0.3, "cm"), legend.key.width = unit(0.8, "cm"))

# --- Panel g: Supervisor Spearman ρ per model (signed values) ---
# Loaded from the raw supervisor benchmark dataset
sup_files <- list.files(results_dir,
                        pattern = "exp07_bench_supervisor_results_.*\\.json",
                        full.names = TRUE)
sup_files <- sup_files[!grepl("latest", basename(sup_files))]
sup_rows <- list()
for (f in sup_files) {
  d <- tryCatch(fix_json(f), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(d) || is.null(d$summary)) next
  for (mk in names(d$summary)) {
    m <- d$summary[[mk]]
    sup_rows[[length(sup_rows) + 1]] <- tibble(
      model = mk,
      rho = m$score_judge_spearman %||% NA_real_,
      n = m$n %||% NA_integer_
    )
  }
}
sup_df <- bind_rows(sup_rows) |>
  filter(!is.na(rho)) |>
  mutate(model = canonical_model_name(model)) |>
  group_by(model) |>
  slice_tail(n = 1) |>   # keep latest entry per model if duplicated across files
  ungroup() |>
  arrange(rho) |>
  mutate(model = factor(model, levels = model),
         is_best = rho == max(rho))

ed3c <- ggplot(sup_df, aes(x = rho, y = model, fill = is_best)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.3, color = "grey40") +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", rho)),
            hjust = -0.15, size = 2.2, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(`TRUE` = col_good, `FALSE` = col_info), guide = "none") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.45), expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))) +
  labs(title = "c",
       subtitle = sprintf("Best Spearman rho = %.2f (%s); n = 200 items per model",
                          max(sup_df$rho),
                          as.character(sup_df$model[which.max(sup_df$rho)])),
       x = "Spearman \u03c1 (supervisor score vs judge correctness)",
       y = NULL) +
  theme_nmi() +
  theme(plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 7, color = "grey40"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 6),
        plot.margin = margin(4, 8, 4, 4))

# --- Combine: accuracy heatmap + latency heatmap + supervisor Spearman ---
ed_fig3 <- ed3a / ed3b / ed3c +
  plot_layout(heights = c(1.4, 1.4, 1.1)) +
  plot_annotation(
    theme = theme_nmi() + theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold", margin = margin(b = 4)))
  )

ed_fig3
save_figure(ed_fig3, "ed_fig4_component_benchmarks", w = 180, h = 170)
} # end if has_v47_data
```

---

# Extended Data Figure 5 — Distiller Prompt Ablation (V4-7.5)

**Purpose**: Show that few-shot examples (F1) provide the largest improvement for distiller
number and source retention, with diminishing returns from combining variants.
Three panels: (a) number retention heatmap, (b) source retention heatmap, (c) marginal
improvement over baseline.

```{r ed-fig4-distiller-ablation, fig.width=7.09, fig.height=9.45}
# --- Load ALL exp07b files and merge ---
abl_files <- list.files(results_dir,
                        pattern = "exp07b_distiller_ablation_.*\\.json$",
                        full.names = TRUE)
# Exclude the "latest" symlink to avoid double-counting
abl_files <- abl_files[!grepl("_latest\\.json$", abl_files)]

variant_levels <- c("baseline", "F1", "F2", "F3", "F1_F3", "F2_F3", "F1_F2_F3")

abl_items <- map_dfr(abl_files, function(f) {
  d <- fix_json(f)
  models <- names(d$results)
  map_dfr(models, function(m) {
    variants <- names(d$results[[m]])
    map_dfr(variants, function(v) {
      items <- d$results[[m]][[v]]$per_item
      if (is.null(items) || length(items) == 0) return(tibble())
      as_tibble(items) |>
        mutate(model = m, variant = v)
    })
  })
})

# Clean model display names (canonical `family:size` format)
abl_items <- abl_items |>
  mutate(
    model_display = canonical_model_name(model),
    variant = factor(variant, levels = variant_levels),
    has_error = !is.na(error) & error != "" & error != "None"
  )

# Filter out error rows and models with >50% error rate on baseline
baseline_errors <- abl_items |>
  filter(variant == "baseline") |>
  group_by(model) |>
  summarise(err_rate = mean(has_error, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

viable_models <- baseline_errors |> filter(err_rate <= 0.5) |> pull(model)
abl_clean <- abl_items |>
  filter(model %in% viable_models, !has_error)

# Deduplicate: keep latest per model×variant×question_id
abl_clean <- abl_clean |>
  group_by(model, model_display, variant, question_id) |>
  slice_tail(n = 1) |>
  ungroup()

n_abl_evals <- nrow(abl_clean)
n_abl_models <- n_distinct(abl_clean$model)
cat(sprintf("Distiller ablation: %d evals, %d models, %d variants\n",
            n_abl_evals, n_abl_models, n_distinct(abl_clean$variant)))

# --- Summary stats per model × variant ---
abl_summary <- abl_clean |>
  group_by(model_display, variant) |>
  summarise(
    mean_nr = mean(number_retention, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_sr = mean(source_retention, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_nr = sd(number_retention, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Baseline values per model
abl_baseline <- abl_summary |>
  filter(variant == "baseline") |>
  select(model_display, baseline_nr = mean_nr, baseline_sr = mean_sr)

abl_summary <- abl_summary |>
  left_join(abl_baseline, by = "model_display") |>
  mutate(delta_nr = mean_nr - baseline_nr,
         delta_sr = mean_sr - baseline_sr)

# Order models by baseline number retention
model_nr_order <- abl_baseline |> arrange(baseline_nr) |> pull(model_display)
abl_summary <- abl_summary |>
  mutate(model_display = factor(model_display, levels = model_nr_order))

# --- Panel a: Number retention heatmap ---
ed4a <- abl_summary |>
  ggplot(aes(x = variant, y = model_display, fill = mean_nr)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.0f%%", mean_nr * 100)),
            size = 2.5, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = col_bad, mid = col_mid, high = col_good,
```
